business.headline: Uday Kotak warns consumers to 'prepare for the worst' amid US-Iran conflictSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Kotak warned that rising energy prices linked to tensions in West Asia could soon begin hurting consumers and businesses, especially those already struggling with limited incomes.

Sensex ends 1,456 points lower, Nifty below 23,400; TCS, Infosys fall over 3%The BSE Sensex crashed 1,456.32 points or 1.92% to close at 74,558.96, while the NSE Nifty50 slumped 454.15 points or 1.91% to settle at 23,361.70.

Tourism body backs PM Modi's travel appeal, seeks more foreign touristsThe industry body said tourism can become a strong source of foreign exchange if the right steps are taken. It suggested easier visa rules, better flight connectivity and stronger global marketing to make India more attractive to international travellers.

Why Indians poured Rs 3 lakh crore into mutual funds despite market jittersIndia's mutual fund industry recorded total net inflows of Rs 3.22 lakh crore in April 2026, while overall Assets Under Management (AUM) climbed to a record Rs 81.92 lakh crore, according to the latest monthly data released by the Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI)..

Gold prices remain steady: Should you sell now or wait?With gold prices showing little movement in recent days, many investors are left wondering what to do next. Is this a pause before another rise, or a good moment to lock in profits and sell?

India declines US-sanctioned Russian LNG despite supply strainIndia has told Russia it will not buy liquefied natural gas cargoes covered by US sanctions. The decision has left a tanker waiting near Singapore and underlined the compliance risks around Russian LNG.

AI can improve portfolios, but good investing still needs context: Geojit's Jones GeorgeAs India's retail investing boom accelerates, AI-driven tools are reshaping how investors access markets and build portfolios. But technology alone cannot replace discipline, judgment and long-term financial understanding, says Jones George, Executive Director at Geojit Financial Services.

Groww shares tumble sharply after block deal: Should investors worry?Groww shares slid sharply after a block deal by early investors followed the lock-in expiry. Analysts said the fall reflected supply pressure and weak markets rather than any fresh business setback.

Sensex crashes over 1,500 points: Why is the stock market falling today?As of 3:13 pm, the Sensex was trading at 74,486.50, down 1,528.78 points or 2.01%, while the Nifty50 declined 458.85 points or 1.93% to 23,357.00.

Infosys, TCS shares tumble up to 4%: Why are IT shares falling today?Indian IT stocks slid sharply on Tuesday as global growth worries and broader market weakness intensified. The selloff underscored concerns over client spending, West Asia tensions and near-term volatility.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,800; Infosys, TCS down over 2%The BSE Sensex fell over 400 points in early trade to 75,611.60, down 403.68 points or 0.53% at 9:18 am. The NSE Nifty50 also slipped below the crucial 23,800 mark, trading at 23,703.65, down 112.20 points or 0.47%.

Rupee sinks to all-time low of 95.58 against US dollar amid crude oil shockThe rupee fell to a record 95.58 against the US dollar as crude prices jumped and Iran-related tensions flared. The slide has deepened concerns over inflation, market volatility and pressure on India's external balances.

PM Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year rattles jewellery marketsPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year has rattled jewellery markets, with traders warning that weaker demand could hurt small jewellers, karigars and thousands of workers dependent on the industry.
Zoho to bring back WFH? Sridhar Vembu responds to PM Modi's appealVembu said Zoho may revisit WFH after PM Modi urged fuel-saving measures to ease economic pressure.

Rs 17.3 lakh schooling, Rs 5.4 lpa salary: Is education becoming a financial trap?Education has long been treated as a non-negotiable expense in Indian households. But today, as costs rise year after year, more families are quietly asking a difficult question: is quality schooling becoming harder to afford?

Petrol, diesel prices may soon go up. How much more could you pay?Petrol and diesel prices could be hiked soon amid surging crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether the likely fuel price hikes would be sharp or gradual over a period of time.

India's best defence is to electrify everything, says Sagar AdaniSagar Adani said India's long-term constraints, water security, food systems, and digital growth, all run into the same bottleneck: reliable, affordable energy.

No petrol or diesel shortage, govt assures day after PM's 'save fuel' appealDuring an inter-ministerial briefing on the situation in West Asia, government officials said India has enough fuel stocks and has taken steps to reduce the impact of the ongoing conflict in the region.

IT employees' union urges govt to back work-from-home following PM's appealIn a letter dated May 11, 2026, NITES urged the Ministry of Labour and Employment to direct IT and digitally deliverable service sectors to implement work-from-home arrangements for a suitable period in the national interest.

Sensex ends 1,313 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; Titan down 7%The BSE Sensex plunged 1,312.91 points, or 1.70%, to close at 76,015.28, while the Nifty 50 fell 360.30 points, or 1.49%, to settle at 23,815.85, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.

entertainment.headline: Who is Sanskruti Jayana, actor playing Satyabhama in Krishnavataram Part 1? Sanskruti Jayana has made her screen debut as Satyabhama in Krishnavataram Part 1: The Heart. Her family background, formal training and early praise have brought the newcomer into focus.

The Rivals of Amziah King trailer: Matthew McConaughey fights to save his family The first trailer for The Rivals of Amziah King shows Matthew McConaughey as a honey-maker and bluegrass musician facing rivals after reconnecting with his estranged foster daughter.

Satrangi trailer: Anshumaan Pushkar's Launda Naach act drives revenge drama The trailer of Satrangi - Badle Ka Khel promises an intense revenge drama set in rural Uttar Pradesh, starring Anshumaan Pushkar in a double role as Bablu and Lalli. The series also explores the traditional folk art of Launda Naach amid caste politics and family rivalry.

Priyanka Chopra shares adorable note given by daughter Malti Marie Priyanka Chopra Jonas shared a handmade Mother's Day note from daughter Malti Marie on social media. The note about her favourite things left the actor emotional as she wrote that her daughter knows her so well.

Viral: Sonu Nigam warns fans after man climbs onstage mid-concert Sonu Nigam reacted after a fan rushed onto the stage during his Kolhapur concert and tried to touch his feet mid-performance. The singer later shared the video online and warned audiences against such risky behaviour.

Aishwarya Rai's absence from L'Oreal Cannes 2026 campaign sparks fan outrage Actor Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's absence from L'Oreal Paris's Cannes 2026 campaign video set off questions online. The reaction reflected her long association with the brand and the Cannes Film Festival.

Amrita Puri defends Deepika Padukone against backlash for 8-hour-shift demand Actor Amrita Puri has defended Deepika Padukone amid the debate over her reported 8-hour shift demand, raising questions about gender bias and working conditions in Bollywood.

Pritam Chakraborty reveals real reason behind Arijit Singh's playback exit Pritam Chakraborty said Arijit Singh's playback singing retirement should be seen as a break. He said the singer needs time away from industry pressure and may return when he wants.

System trailer: Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika team up for high-stakes legal fight The trailer of System offers a gripping glimpse into a courtroom battle where justice clashes with power and influence. Starring Sonakshi Sinha, Jyotika, and Ashutosh Gowariker, the film premieres on Prime Video on May 22.

Hayden Panettiere recalls being forced into bed with 'undressed' famous man at 18 Actor-singer Hayden Panettiere recalled how, when she was 18, a woman she trusted took her to a room on a boat where an undressed, famous man waited. The account features in her memoir, published on May 12, 2026.

Trisha may join Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan in Nelson's film as female lead Actor Trisha Krishnan is reportedly in talks to play a key role, possibly the female lead, in Nelson Dilipkumar's film starring Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan. The prospect has intensified attention on one of Tamil cinema's most closely watched projects.

Remarkably Bright Creatures: Author Shelby Explains the Ending, Tova, Sally Field, Lewis Pullman In this exclusive interview with India Today's Bhavna Agarwal (@filmybugB), bestselling author Shelby Van Pelt opens up about writing her debut novel Remarkably Bright Creatures, the inspiration behind Marcellus the octopus, and how her Swedish grandmother inspired the beloved character Tova.

BTS are this generation's Michael Jackson and The Beatles. Mexico proved it BTS' fan frenzy is being compared to that of The Beatles in the 60s and Michael Jackson in 80-90s. Their recent Mexico City takeover proved why the comparisons are not exaggerated.

The Rings of Power renewed for Season 4 ahead of Season 3 Prime Video has renewed The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power for a fourth season and announced the premiere date of the third season. The early renewal follows strong global viewership, while the next chapter shifts to the War of the Elves and Sauron.

Telugu actor Seerat Kapoor calls out misogynistic Allu Arjun property remark Telugu actor Seerat Kapoor reacted after an Instagram user called her "Allu Arjun property". The actor's sharp response has now sparked widespread conversation online.

Tara Sutaria's stylish airport look sparks Cannes 2026 departure buzz Actor Tara Sutaria was photographed at the airport in a sharply tailored pinstripe suit. Her appearance has added to the growing buzz around India's presence at the film festival.

The rights are with me: Amish Tripathi shuts down Ranveer Singh-Meluha reports Ranveer Singh has reportedly acquired Amish Tripathi's The Immortals of Meluha to develop it with Birla Studios. The project is in the writing stage, with the actor also expected to play Lord Shiva as the makers shape a long-term franchise.

Actor Suriya stops security to help fan get a selfie at Karuppu event. Watch At the Veerabhadrudu (Karuppu in Tamil) pre-release event, Suriya stopped security and called back a fan who ran up for a hug and selfie. The clip quickly went viral, drawing attention to the actor's gesture ahead of the film's release.

Cannes 2026: Chardikala to Balan, regional films dominate India's entries at fest Indian regional cinema is set to make a strong impact at Cannes 2026 with films like Chardikala, Balan: The Boy and September 21 screening across various sections of the festival. Read to know more details.

general news.headline: NEET-UG held on May 3 cancelled after Rajasthan paper leak, CBI probe orderedThe NEET-UG 2026 exam, which was conducted on May 3, has been cancelled over allegations of a paper leak. The government has ordered a CBI probe into the allegations with the National Testing Agency saying dates for the re-exam will be announced soon.

Vijay's astrologer, Radhan Pandit Vettrivel, gets key post in his governmentVijay's astrologer Radhan Pandit Vettrivel, credited with predicting the victory of TVK, has been appointed Officer on Special Duty (Political) to the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister after the party's electoral win. His close association with several political leaders and his influence on key decisions has drawn attention.

NEET-UG cancelled: Super 30's Anand Kumar wants China-like crackdown after leakAnand Kumar called for strict action against coaching centres after NEET-UG 2026 was cancelled over alleged irregularities. The decision has triggered a re-test and a CBI probe into the examination process.

Stalin can pull strings in Vijay govt, but TVK has a plan BVijay formed the government in Tamil Nadu with outside support from DMK allies who still swear allegiance to Stalin. The arrangement gives him a floor-test buffer, but it leaves the door open for Stalin to pull strings in the TVK government. But Vijay seems to have a backup in place.

An RSS whisper and beauty parlour didis: How BJP swept Bengal with micro campaignsWhile Narendra Modi and Amit Shah's rallies acted as booster shots in West Bengal, RSS affiliates and the BJP's booth-level workers carried out a year-long whisper campaign. They used auto drivers and beauty parlour didis as local influencers, and turned the anxiety and resentment around Mamata Banerjee's rule into a saffron tsunami for the BJP.

Live: Students, parents react to exam shock and uncertaintyNEET UG 2026 cancelled Live Updates: NEET UG 2026 has been cancelled after a suspected paper leak triggered a nationwide controversy, affecting over 22 lakh students. The government has ordered a CBI probe, while NTA confirmed a re-exam will be held soon, with no fresh registration required and revised dates to be announced.

Makes Mahabharat look small: Supreme Court as Sanjay Kapur estate row heats upA fresh Supreme Court plea, allegations of forged documents, a battle over control of the RK Family Trust and a sharp courtroom remark have pushed the Sunjay Kapur family dispute into a dramatic new phase.

Dravid spends crores on a cricket team, but won't even attend practice. Here's whyRahul Dravid has been unveiled as Dublin Guardians owner in the European T20 Premier League. The move completes the six-team league structure ahead of the tournament's 2026 launch.
Alia Bhatt looks like a summer dream in first look from Cannes 2026Alia Bhatt is back in Cannes, and her first appearance this year is already serving major summer holiday fashion inspiration.

NEET UG 2026 cancelled: 'Leak-proof' fixes by Digvijay Singh panel which NTA ignoredNTA cancelled NEET UG 2026 after allegations that a circulated guess paper matched the exam. The move has renewed scrutiny of parliamentary warnings that exam security reforms were not fully implemented.

From US to Iran, why is Saudi blaming everyone at once now?Saudi Arabia's refusal to back Trump's Hormuz military plan has exposed growing cracks with Washington, while widening tensions with the UAE over Iran, oil routes and the future balance of power in the Gulf.

How made-in-India assault rifles are saluting defence hero Manohar ParrikarManohar Gopalakrishna Parrikar's thrust on indigenisation of defence products and reforms opened doors for private players in the sector. Today, the former defence minister's legacy lives on in the M72, G72 and P72 assault rifles, named after his initials. Fully made in India, the rifles are now being used by India's security forces.

CCTV clips of 'mechanic' with hidden sickle knocking on doors spark fearA man, who poses as a mechanic or delivery worker, has triggered panic in Pitampura and Keshav Puram by ringing doorbells and attempting to enter homes. CCTV footage has repeatedly captured him, but police are still searching for the suspect after linking him to an earlier robbery.

How CM Vijay's empire stands on the tombs of an alliance and a partyTVK chief C Jospeh Vijay's rise to power in Tamil Nadu has fractured the two Dravidian parties, the DMK and the AIADMK, and the alliances led by them. Then there is the rupture within the ranks of the AIADMK, which has already suffered an OPS-led split.

Beyond politics: Actor Simran reacts to Vijay meeting Stalin as Tamil Nadu CMActor Simran praised Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay's meeting with DMK president MK Stalin after the two leaders met at Stalin's residence in Chennai. Her post cast the courtesy call as a display of warmth, dignity and Tamil Nadu's democratic spirit.

Is poha an unhealthy breakfast option? Sanjeev Kapoor says yesChef Sanjeev Kapoor recently spoke about why poha - despite being one of India's most popular comfort breakfasts - may come with certain nutritional concerns.

India turning water into fuel, China into technology, and a crisis is brewingOne is doubling down on the farm, while the other is betting on its factories. That choice may define which nation holds the economic upper hand in the decades ahead.

Are Delhi NCR schools and universities going online? Possibility but no confirmationDelhi schools and universities are preparing to shift classes online after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged institutions to reduce fuel consumption amid rising global energy concerns. With no official mandate yet, many schools are voluntarily planning hybrid or online schedules to cut transport fuel use while ensuring learning continues without disruption.

NEET UG 2026 collapsed in just 9 days. Here's what happenedThe NTA cancelled NEET UG 2026 after complaints that a circulated question bank resembled the paper. The move followed a widening probe, detentions and renewed scrutiny of exam integrity.

Udhayanidhi Stalin repeats controversial comments on Sanatan, CM Vijay watchesUdhayanidhi Stalin repeated his call for the eradication of Sanatanam in the Tamil Nadu Assembly. The remark drew fresh BJP attacks, while Assembly immunity limits the immediate legal risk.

Aishwarya Rai's absence from L'Oreal Cannes 2026 campaign sparks fan outrageActor Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's absence from L'Oreal Paris's Cannes 2026 campaign video set off questions online. The reaction reflected her long association with the brand and the Cannes Film Festival.

Bengali Shaadi Mein Abdulla DeewanaThe despondency of the Non-Resident Bengalis or NRBs doesn't get reflected in the celebrations in West Bengal. The state no longer sticks out like a special sore thumb.

Day after PM's save fuel appeal, BJP leader travels to Bhopal in 50-car convoyBJP leader Saubhagya Singh Thakur travelled from Ujjain to Bhopal on Monday to formally take charge as chairman of the Madhya Pradesh Textbook Corporation. However, it was not merely the appointment ceremony that drew attention, but the scale of the convoy accompanying him to the state capital.

The fridge magnets telling travel stories in Indian homesFridge magnets are emerging as favoured travel souvenirs in Indian homes, carrying memories from trips and gifts from loved ones. Their appeal lies in turning refrigerators into personal travel archives, of milestones, and nostalgia.

sports.headline: MS Dhoni to play in Europe? Abhishek Bachchan reveals his big ETPL wish Abhishek Bachchan said he would love to see MS Dhoni play in the European T20 Premier League. His wish also exposed the BCCI rule that keeps contracted Indian players out of overseas leagues.

Virat Kohli jokes about twin ducks in hilarious dressing room spech: Video Virat Kohli laughed off his recent batting slump with a cheeky dressing room joke after RCB's win over Mumbai Indians, showing little concern as Bengaluru push for a top-two IPL finish.

Dravid spends crores on a cricket team, but won't even attend practice. Here's why Rahul Dravid has been unveiled as Dublin Guardians owner in the European T20 Premier League. The move completes the six-team league structure ahead of the tournament's 2026 launch.

Shabnim reverses retirement, returns to South Africa Women's T20 World Cup squad South Africa have received a massive boost ahead of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 after veteran fast bowler Shabnim Ismail reversed her international retirement to return to the Proteas squad for the marquee tournament in England and Wales.

Sakshee Malikkh rallies behind Vinesh Phogat: Don't block a mother's right to compete Olympic medalist Sakshee Malikkh hit out at the WFI for stalling Vinesh Phogat's historic return to wrestling. Citing global support for female athletes, she urged the government to remove bureaucratic barriers against the new mother.

Wrestler's protest cost athletes international exposure: Deepak Punia Deepak Punia said the disruption after the wrestlers' protest reduced overseas competition for Indian wrestlers. He said the fallout affected preparation, selection opportunities and long-term progress.

Ochoa's last World Cup mission? Mexican great targets Messi, Ronaldo milestone Veteran Mexico goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa is on the verge of creating football history as the 40-year-old closes in on a sixth FIFA World Cup appearance in 2026, a rare feat achieved only by Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo.

Meet Rishabh Yadav: The boy who wants to give Indian archery its greatest day Rishabh Yadav has set his sights on LA 2028 after a breakout 2025 in compound archery. His rise, and his world-record pairing with Jyothi Surekha Vennam, has strengthened India's hope of an overdue Olympic medal.

Rahul Dravid backs bowler-friendly pitches to restore balance in T20s Former India coach Rahul Dravid said T20 cricket needs more challenging pitches to give bowlers a fairer chance, warning that the format risks becoming too heavily skewed towards batters because of the dramatic evolution in modern-day hitting.

Does Virat Kohli fake aggression for cameras? Manjrekar offers ringside view Sanjay Manjrekar said he is surprised Virat Kohli still carries the same fire and energy at 37, adding that the star batter's emotional intensity and sensitivity to criticism have long fuelled his game.

Is Shreyas Iyer annoyed by PBKS' bowlers? Numbers and his reaction say so Shreyas Iyer's blunt post-match reaction reflected Punjab Kings' growing bowling crisis, with PBKS conceding 200-plus totals seven times this IPL season despite staying in the playoff hunt.

French Open: Stan Wawrinka, Gael Monfils given wildcard entries in Roland GarrosFrench Open organisers have handed wildcard entries to Stan Wawrinka and Gael Monfils for Roland Garros, giving the two veteran stars one final chance to compete at a tournament that shaped their remarkable careers.

No Neeraj Chopra in Rome entry list, Sachin Yadav set for Diamond League debutNeeraj Chopra is set to miss the Rome Diamond League as Sachin Yadav prepares for his debut. Chopra's absence shifts focus to Yadav, who enters a stacked field after his Tokyo breakthrough.

IPL 2026: Axar Patel fined for slow over-rate after PBKS vs DCAxar Patel was fined Rs 12 lakh after Delhi Capitals maintained a slow over-rate against Punjab Kings in Dharamshala. The sanction came as Delhi kept their IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive with a three-wicket win.

IPL, GT vs SRH: Gujarat Titans host SRH as playoff race enters decisive phaseIPL, GT vs SRH: Third-placed Gujarat Titans host second-placed Sunrisers Hyderabad in a high-stakes IPL 2026 clash in Ahmedabad on Tuesday, with the playoff race heating up and the battle for a top-two finish gathering pace.

IPL playoff scenario: Punjab Kings enter danger zone, Delhi Capitals cling onDC vs PBKS: The race for the playoffs is heating up with every passing match. On Monday, Delhi Capitals somehow kept their qualification hopes alive with a thrilling three-wicket win over Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings in Dharamsala.

No mat, no mercy: Vinesh Phogat vs wrestling federation latest episode explainedThe dispute between Vinesh Phogat and the Wrestling Federation of India has evolved into a major governance battle. As both sides continue to defend their positions, the standoff has raised larger questions about how international clearances and domestic federation regulations intersect in Indian sport.

PBKS bowling sways in Dharamshala's winds, hurts playoff hopesPBKS vs DC: Punjab Kings' bowling collapse, marked by poor execution, costly extras and missed chances, allowed Delhi Capitals to recover from 33/3 and pull off a record chase in Dharamshala.

Axar Patel, David Miller redemption party keeps DC afloat in Dharamshala thrillerIPL 2026: Axar Patel and David Miller finally delivered when Delhi Capitals needed them the most. Their fearless counterattack helped DC pull off a record chase in Dharamshala and keep their fading IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive.

Rahul Dravid enters European T20 Premier league, buys Dublin Guardians franchiseRahul Dravid has joined ETPL as owner of the Dublin Guardians before the league's 2026 launch. His move underlines Europe's latest attempt to build a serious franchise T20 market.

The voice of the heartland: How Bhojpuri found its home in the IPLIPL 2026: Robin Singh has become one of the biggest faces of Bhojpuri commentary in the IPL. But beyond the microphone, the former coach and talent scout is also helping Bihar's next generation of cricketers find a path to the biggest stage.

Tim David fined for obscene gesture during RCB's last-ball thriller vs MITim David was fined and given two demerit points after an obscene gesture during RCB's win over Mumbai Indians. The sanction capped a chaotic last-ball contest that boosted Bengaluru's playoff push and hurt Mumbai's chances.

25-0: Jannik Sinner keeps cooking before French Open with another historic featJannik Sinner crushed Alexei Popyrin 6-2, 6-0 at the Italian Open to extend his unbeaten Masters 1000 streak to 30 matches and become the first player to start 25-0 across the season's first five Masters events.

Neymar's FIFA World Cup 2026 dream gets major boost with Brazil squad returnCarlo Ancelotti has included Neymar in Brazil's preliminary 55-man squad after his return with Santos. The call-up puts him back in contention for the 2026 World Cup, provided he stays fit and maintains form.

Salman Agha challenges Bangladesh in Mirpur Test: Set Pakistan 260 if brave enoughBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Salman Agha has challenged Bangladesh to set a target of around 260 in 70 overs, insisting Pakistan would go all out for victory in the opening Test at Mirpur.

Who is Madhav Tiwari? DC's fast-bowling all-rounder who troubled Priyansh AryaYoung fast-bowling all-rounder Madhav Tiwari announced himself in IPL 2026 with the prized wicket of Priyansh Arya during Delhi Capitals' crucial clash against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala on Monday.

India's FIFA U17 World Cup dream ends after 0-3 loss to China in Asian Cup QFIndia's historic FIFA U17 Women's World Cup dream came to an end after a quarterfinal defeat to hosts China in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup. Despite the loss, the Young Tigresses created history by reaching the knockout stage for the first time.

Coco Gauff escapes huge scare before French Open with unreal Rome comebackItalian Open 2026: Defending French Open champion Coco Gauff saved a match point before storming back to beat teenage sensation Iva Jovic in a dramatic clash in Rome. The American rallied from 5-7, 3-5, 30-40 down to keep her Roland Garros preparations on track.

Kuldeep Yadav among 5 dropped by DC for PBKS clash as playoff hopes fade awayIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals dropped Kuldeep Yadav and made five changes for the Punjab Kings match in Dharamshala. The decision exposed Delhi's tactical reset as Punjab tried to steady their top-four push.

Liverpool honour Diogo Jota and his brother with new Anfield memorial sculptureLiverpool FC have announced a permanent tribute to late forward Diogo Jota and his brother Andre Silva at Anfield, honouring the two Portuguese brothers who died in a car accident last year.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
